name: Logarithm Calculator
description: Industrial-grade logarithmic calculator for process scaling, decibel analysis, and logarithmic trend decomposition in manufacturing and logistics. Compliant with ISO 80000-2, Lean Six Sigma, and WERC DC Metrics.

inputs.label: Value X (operand)
inputs.businessContext: Primary input for logarithmic calculation. Represents throughput, defect count, or signal amplitude.

businessContext_i18n.en: Primary input for logarithmic calculation. Represents throughput, defect count, or signal amplitude.

inputs.label: Logarithm Base
inputs.businessContext: Select logarithmic base per application: base 2 for binary entropy, base e for continuous processes, base 10 for decibel and pH scales.

businessContext_i18n.en: Select logarithmic base per application: base 2 for binary entropy, base e for continuous processes, base 10 for decibel and pH scales.

inputs.label: Unit Type
inputs.businessContext: Select unit type to apply correct scaling for decibel calculations in acoustics, electronics, or vibration analysis.

businessContext_i18n.en: Select unit type to apply correct scaling for decibel calculations in acoustics, electronics, or vibration analysis.

inputs.label: Reference Value (for ratio)
inputs.businessContext: Reference baseline for ratio-based logarithmic calculations (e.g., reference power, reference amplitude).

businessContext_i18n.en: Reference baseline for ratio-based logarithmic calculations (e.g., reference power, reference amplitude).

inputs.label: Enable Threshold Alerts
inputs.businessContext: Toggle to activate automated alerts when logarithmic results exceed predefined industrial thresholds.

businessContext_i18n.en: Toggle to activate automated alerts when logarithmic results exceed predefined industrial thresholds.

rules.message: Value X must be greater than zero (logarithm undefined for non-positive numbers).
Reference value must be greater than zero.
Natural log of very large numbers may cause numerical overflow; consider scaling.

thresholds.message: Logarithmic result exceeds 100 — possible data anomaly or extreme scaling.
thresholds.recommendedAction: Verify input values and unit type. Consider using a smaller base or normalizing data.

thresholds.message: Logarithmic result below -100 — extremely small ratio detected.
thresholds.recommendedAction: Check if reference value is appropriate. May indicate near-zero signal.

thresholds.message: Decibel level exceeds 120 dB — potential hearing damage or equipment overload per OSHA/ISO 1999.
thresholds.recommendedAction: Immediately review source levels and implement engineering controls.

formulas.name: Raw Logarithm
formulas.description: Compute logarithm of value_x with selected base.
formulas.formula: if base == 'e': ln(value_x); elif base == '2': log2(value_x); else: log10(value_x)

formulas.name: Logarithm of Ratio
formulas.description: Compute logarithm of (value_x / reference_value) with selected base.
formulas.formula: if base == 'e': ln(value_x / reference_value); elif base == '2': log2(value_x / reference_value); else: log10(value_x / reference_value)

formulas.name: Decibel (Power Ratio)
formulas.description: Convert power ratio to decibels: 10 * log10(P1/P0).

formulas.name: Decibel (Amplitude Ratio)
formulas.description: Convert amplitude ratio to decibels: 20 * log10(A1/A0).

formulas.name: Entropy Contribution (Shannon)
formulas.description: Compute -p * log2(p) for information entropy (p = value_x / sum).

formulas.name: Continuous Growth Rate
formulas.description: Natural log of ratio as instantaneous growth rate (e.g., for Six Sigma process shift).

formulas.name: Primary Logarithmic Result
formulas.description: Final primary output based on unit type and base selection. Defaults to raw logarithm if dimensionless.
formulas.formula: if unit_type == 'power_ratio': decibel_power; elif unit_type == 'amplitude_ratio': decibel_amplitude; else: log_raw

primary.label: Primary Logarithmic Result
primary.description: Final calculated logarithmic value based on inputs and unit type.

logRaw.label: Raw Logarithm
logRaw.description: Logarithm of value_x without ratio scaling.

growthRate.label: Continuous Growth Rate
growthRate.description: Instantaneous growth rate from natural log.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Numerical Precision Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Extreme ratios (>1e6 or <1e-6) may cause floating-point precision errors in logarithmic calculation.
hiddenLossDrivers.mitigation: Use double-precision or scale inputs to moderate range.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Base Mismatch in Decibel Context
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Using base 2 or e for decibel calculations may lead to misinterpretation; standard decibel uses base 10.
hiddenLossDrivers.mitigation: Ensure unit type 'power_ratio' or 'amplitude_ratio' is paired with base 10.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Reference Value Drift
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If reference value changes over time (e.g., aging sensor baseline), logarithmic ratios become unreliable.

suggestedActions.label: Scale Inputs to Moderate Range
suggestedActions.description: If value_x or reference_value are extremely large/small, rescale to range [0.001, 1000] to improve numerical stability.

suggestedActions.label: Verify Unit Type Selection
suggestedActions.description: Confirm that unit type matches the physical quantity: power ratio (dB = 10log10) vs amplitude ratio (dB = 20log10).

suggestedActions.label: Use Base 10 for Decibel Calculations
suggestedActions.description: For acoustic or electronic decibel measurements, always select base 10 to comply with IEC 60027-3.

suggestedActions.label: Perform Entropy Analysis for Process Variation
suggestedActions.description: If base 2 selected, use entropy contribution to quantify information content in defect distribution (Lean Six Sigma).

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Result
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Primary result adjusted for data quality and hidden loss drivers. Scale factor between 0.8 and 1.2 applied based on input range and threshold violations.
dataConfidenceAdjusted.calculation: primaryResult * (1 - 0.1 * (number of active high-severity hidden losses) + 0.05 * (if all inputs within recommended range))
